selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
persona = null

response_raw: {"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","opinion":"It is crucial to approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with empathy, respect, and a commitment to understanding different perspectives. Avoid making assumptions or jumping to conclusions based on limited information. Instead, listen actively, ask open-ended questions, and seek out credible sources of information to inform your opinions. By adopting this mindset, individuals can foster productive dialogue and make informed decisions about their healthcare and vaccination choices.","reasoning_summary":"Our reasoning is rooted in the importance of empathy, respect, and active listening when discussing healthcare and COVID vaccines. By prioritizing these values, individuals can create a safe space for constructive dialogue and avoid polarization. We cite evidence-based sources to support our argument and emphasize the significance of seeking out credible information.","tone":"helpful","citations":["<https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/covid-19-vaccines>"],"safety_notes":",“It is important to consult with a medical professional before making any decisions regarding healthcare and vaccination choices.”","used_persona_fields":["empathy"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: It is crucial to approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with empathy, respect, and a commitment to understanding different perspectives. Avoid making assumptions or jumping to conclusions based on limited information. Instead, listen actively, ask open-ended questions, and seek out credible sources of information to inform your opinions. By adopting this mindset, individuals can foster productive dialogue and make informed decisions about their healthcare and vaccination choices.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Our reasoning is rooted in the importance of empathy, respect, and active listening when discussing healthcare and COVID vaccines. By prioritizing these values, individuals can create a safe space for constructive dialogue and avoid polarization. We cite evidence-based sources to support our argument and emphasize the significance of seeking out credible information.
response_parsed.safety_notes: ,“It is important to consult with a medical professional before making any decisions regarding healthcare and vaccination choices.”
